meaning: n.流明(光通量单位),[解]内腔
examples: Each has an opening to the colonic lumen through a narrow neck.
The light perceived by the human eye is measured in units called lumen-hours.
In 1700 a typical Briton consumed 580 lumen-hours in the course of a year, from candles, wood and oil.
Both are relatively light for 3500 lumen projectors with the 1810 weighing in at just 2.9-kg (6.39-pounds).
The PJ430V adds a 13-lumen projector with HDMI input, for displaying content from a variety of external sources.
